pick.rationale: Louisville holds a significant analytical edge with superior KenPom (18 vs 50) and Torvik (16 vs 50) rankings, featuring balanced efficiency on both ends of the floor. However, the absence of star freshman Mikel Brown Jr. (18 ppg) due to a back injury substantially narrows the gap against a South Florida team riding a 12-game winning streak with conference Player of the Year Izaiyah Nelson. While South Florida's momentum and matchup advantages make this a credible upset scenario, Louisville's overall talent depth and defensive capability should still prevail in a competitive 6/11 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Louisville vs South Florida Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Louisville favored analytically but significantly impacted by Mikel Brown Jr.'s injury against a hot South Florida team.
evidence: Louisville ranks 18th in KenPom and 16th in Torvik vs South Florida's 50th in both systems.
Louisville's adjusted offensive efficiency ranks 20th (KenPom) and 23rd (Torvik), defensive efficiency ranks 23rd (Torvik).
South Florida is on a 12-game winning streak as American Conference regular season and tournament champions.
Louisville will be without star freshman Mikel Brown Jr. (18 ppg) for the first two rounds due to a back injury.
South Florida features conference Player of the Year Izaiyah Nelson averaging near 20-point double-double.
ESPN Analytics gives Louisville an 80.9% win probability despite the injury concerns.
Betting lines show Louisville as 4.5-point favorites, suggesting the market acknowledges the injury impact.

result.text: Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr. for March Madness seed Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Louisville basketball can blame Mikel Brown Jr.'s injury for March Madness seed C.L. Brown Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament and gives an update on his recovery for the NCAA Tournament. Louisville basketball received a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. The Cardinals rated well in many key metrics, but they went just 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury. UofL will need its star point guard healthy to navigate its challenging path for a deep March Madness run. Louisville basketball , in a season of many what-ifs, just had another on Selection Sunday with the NCAA’s release of the tournament bracket . The Cardinals received a No. 6 seed in the East Region and will face No. 11-seed and American Conference champion South Florida on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. in the first round. That’s at least one seed line higher than they should be. As much as the fate of how long they last in the nation’s best championship tournament will be determined by the availability of star freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. , their position as a 6-seed can be attributed to him too. Through no fault of his own, the back injury that forced Brown to miss 10 regular-season games and kept him sidelined for the entire ACC Tournament cost UofL from having a better seed and a slightly easier path to advancing. Brown has done everything he can to play, even trying to play hurt through the pain. After he did so in UofL's loss to Clemson, coach Pat Kelsey said it was best to shut him down until he could come back fully healthy. Brown told The Courier Journal after Louisville's ACC Tournament win over SMU that it's been a childhood dream to play in the NCAA Tournament, which he called "the best basketball (atmosphere) in the world." "I'm not going to just sit out for no reason," Brown said. "I want to play with these guys, I want to be out there, I want to play for the team, but my health is first." Brown's absence was costly in terms of the hypothetical games the Cards perhaps could have won had he played. And it was costly in terms of the reality that his status was a topic of conversation by the NCAA Selection Committee when it began constructing the bracket. result.text: Mikel Brown Jr injury update: Louisville star out first two rounds Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Mikel Brown Jr. to miss Louisville's first round March Madness game vs South Florida Matthew Glenesk USA TODAY Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville vs South Florida prediction for Mikel Brown Jr. status While certain coaches have different rules, former Bellarmine basketball coach says if a player can play, they should. Louisville basketball will be without star freshman Mikel Brown Jr. for its Men's NCAA Tournament first round game against 11-seed South Florida on Thursday in Buffalo. If the Cardinals are to win, Brown will also miss Saturday's second round game, the school announced Wednesday . The winner of Louisville/South Florida plays the winner of Michigan State/North Dakota State. Upset alert: Ranking 8 most likely upsets in March Madness first round Expert picks: Predictions for 2026 March Madness bracket Brown has been dealing with a nagging back injury, but Cardinals coach Pat Kelsey said Monday, March 16 that Brown had “made great progress” leading up to this week. Brown, a projected 2026 NBA draft lottery pick, averaged 18.2 points and 4.7 assists per game this season, but last played Feb. 28. He's missed 11 games this season. It's a major blow for the Cardinals, who are reliant on the star point guard. Louisville enters March Madness with a 7-5 record with Brown on the bench vs. a 16-5 record when he plays. Per CBBAnalytics.com , it averages 6.4 fewer points per 40 minutes (88.1 vs. 81.7) and shoots 4.8% worse from beyond the arc (38.2% vs. 33.4%) when he's not on the court. Kelsey said Brown reaggravated the back injury, which initially surfaced in mid-December, by taking some hard falls during a Feb. 23 loss at then-No. 19 North Carolina. Brown told reporters at the ACC Tournament he was "starting to get there. I'm slowly progressing; I'm slowly doing more day in and day out, just trying to get a feel for it. But, obviously, like coach said, the plan is: I'll come back when I'm 100%." When asked, "Are you 100% positive that you’ll be back for the NCAA Tournament?" He replied, "Right now, I’m just focused on my rehab more than anything. When the time feels right, I'm going to talk to the coaching staff; and we’re going to come to an agreement." result.text: USF basketball picked to beat Louisville in March Madness in upset Skip to main content Home HT Preps College Pro Columns SportsbookWire MMA Junkie Golf Week Why South Florida basketball a popular upset pick in NCAA brackets Kirkland Crawford USA TODAY NETWORK - Florida Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness Final Four predictions and national champion picks From chalk favorites to hot upsets, the panel shares Final Four picks and bold calls on who cuts down the nets. From the first moments that the men's NCAA tournament brackets were unveiled, South Florida basketball has been a chic pick for an upset in the first round . The Bulls, the 11th seed in the East, will get their chance to against No. 6 seed Louisville in Buffalo, New York, on Thursday . USF, the American regular season and tournament champs, is riding a 12-game winning streak. Plus, they have the conference's player of the year in 6-foot-10 Izaiyah Nelson, who is averaging a near-20-point double-double. Watch USF vs. Louisville in NCAA tournament on Sling Meanwhile, the Cardinals have a big question mark in their backcourt. Mikel Brown Jr., a freshman averaging over 18 points per game, has been hobbled by a back injury and isn't a sure bet to play in the tournament. Two major college basketball entities have released their picks for every game throughout the tournament, and USF was a popular pick. ESPN's Jay Bilas picks USF over Louisville Perhaps the preeminent voice in college basketball is ESPN analyst Jay Bilas. He laid out his predictions for every single game in the tournament — not just the first round, but all the way to the championship game based on his picks. READY TO WIN MARCH MADNESS? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! In this USF-Louisville matchup, Bilas favors the Bulls . "One thing is for sure in this game: There will be a lot of long rebounds because both teams like to shoot 3s," Bilas wrote. "This is a great upset pick: Mikel Brown Jr. has been out because of a sore back, and South Florida is tough. It's worth rolling the dice on this one because Michigan State will likely beat either team." Sure enough, Bilas has MSU taking down USF for a spot in the Sweet 16. THINK YOU CAN BEAT OUR EXPERT? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today CBS Sports' Isaac Trotter picks USF over Louisville CBS has been one of the longtime homes of the NCAA tournament, and one of its sports arms' writers, Isaac Trotter, has the Bulls upsetting the Cardinals in Round 1, too . "South Florida's personnel is really good. Joseph Pinion and Wes Enis are outstanding snipers," Trotter said. "Big man Izaiyah Nelson is a two-way menace. South Florida has won 19 of its last 22 games, and two of the three losses came in overtime and the other was by one point. It is not far away from being 22-0. South Florida matches up really, really well with Louisville across the board. It can out-rebound the Cardinals. It can shoot with the Cardinals. It can guard these actions." Brown Louisville Courier Journal Show Caption Hide Caption Louisville basketball's Mikel Brown Jr. provides back injury update Mikel Brown Jr. breaks down the back injury keeping him out of the ACC Tournament and gives an update on his recovery for the NCAA Tournament. Louisville basketball received a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament on Selection Sunday. The Cardinals rated well in many key metrics, but they went just 7-5 in games Mikel Brown Jr. missed due to injury. UofL will need its star point guard healthy to navigate its challenging path for a deep March Madness run. Louisville basketball , in a season of many what-ifs, just had another on Selection Sunday with the NCAA’s release of the tournament bracket . The Cardinals received a No. 6 seed in the East Region and will face No. 11-seed and American Conference champion South Florida on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. in the first round. That’s at least one seed line higher than they should be. As much as the fate of how long they last in the nation’s best championship tournament will be determined by the availability of star freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. , their position as a 6-seed can be attributed to him too. Through no fault of his own, the back injury that forced Brown to miss 10 regular-season games and kept him sidelined for the entire ACC Tournament cost UofL from having a better seed and a slightly easier path to advancing. Brown has done everything he can to play, even trying to play hurt through the pain. After he did so in UofL's loss to Clemson, coach Pat Kelsey said it was best to shut him down until he could come back fully healthy. Brown told The Courier Journal after Louisville's ACC Tournament win over SMU that it's been a childhood dream to play in the NCAA Tournament, which he called "the best basketball (atmosphere) in the world." "I'm not going to just sit out for no reason," Brown said. "I want to play with these guys, I want to be out there, I want to play for the team, but my health is first." Brown's absence was costly in terms of the hypothetical games the Cards perhaps could have won had he played. And it was costly in terms of the reality that his status was a topic of conversation by the NCAA Selection Committee when it began constructing the bracket. From the first moments that the men's NCAA tournament brackets were unveiled, South Florida basketball has been a chic pick for an upset in the first round . The Bulls, the 11th seed in the East, will get their chance to against No. 6 seed Louisville in Buffalo, New York, on Thursday . USF, the American regular season and tournament champs, is riding a 12-game winning streak. Plus, they have the conference's player of the year in 6-foot-10 Izaiyah Nelson, who is averaging a near-20-point double-double. Watch USF vs. Louisville in NCAA tournament on Sling Meanwhile, the Cardinals have a big question mark in their backcourt. Mikel Brown Jr., a freshman averaging over 18 points per game, has been hobbled by a back injury and isn't a sure bet to play in the tournament. Two major college basketball entities have released their picks for every game throughout the tournament, and USF was a popular pick. ESPN's Jay Bilas picks USF over Louisville Perhaps the preeminent voice in college basketball is ESPN analyst Jay Bilas. He laid out his predictions for every single game in the tournament — not just the first round, but all the way to the championship game based on his picks. READY TO WIN MARCH MADNESS? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! In this USF-Louisville matchup, Bilas favors the Bulls . "South Florida's personnel is really good. Joseph Pinion and Wes Enis are outstanding snipers," Trotter said. "Big man Izaiyah Nelson is a two-way menace. South Florida has won 19 of its last 22 games, and two of the three losses came in overtime and the other was by one point. It is not far away from being 22-0. South Florida matches up really, really well with Louisville across the board. It can out-rebound the Cardinals. It can shoot with the Cardinals. It can guard these actions."
